title: A Club Where Lions Dance and Traditions Take Root
subtitle: 舞狮俱乐部：传统与青春的传承

paragraphs.en: The first thing you notice in the staircase to the fourth-floor studio on Canal Street in Manhattan, New York, is the measured thumping coming from behind a metal door. Just beyond the entrance, large lion masks were twisting and turning to the drum-beat. On a recent Friday evening, the teenagers made their way across the studio floor as they practiced Chinese lion dancing.
paragraphs.zh: 走上纽约曼哈顿坚尼街通往四楼工作室的楼梯，首先引起你注意的，是金属门后传来的有节奏的嘭嘭重击声。穿过入口，巨大的狮子头面具正随着鼓点扭动翻转。最近一个周五的傍晚，一群青少年正在工作室里来回穿梭，练习着中国舞狮。

words.meaning: adj. 有节奏的；缓慢而稳重的

paragraphs.en: "You want to play in a circle," Victor Fong, aged 24, told his students at the New York Chinese Freemasons Athletic Club. "Take it slow and do it again." The dance group, made up of 60 members, performs throughout the year but was now preparing for its biggest events Chinese Lunar New Year celebrations, which will begin on Saturday and conclude on February 15. Teenagers comprise about half of the group, many of whom began lion dancing at the age of 14. About 100 current and past members of the dance group — which has been performing since 1956 — will be among the 220 groups marching through Chinatown in Manhattan for the 18th Lunar New Year Parade on February 5.
paragraphs.zh: "你们要走成一个圆，"24岁的维克多·方在纽约华人共济会体育俱乐部对他的学生们说。"慢慢来，再做一遍。"这个由60名成员组成的舞狮团全年都有表演，但眼下正在为最盛大的活动——农历新年庆典做准备，庆典将于周六开始，持续至2月15日。团里大约一半是青少年，其中许多人从14岁起就开始舞狮。这支自1956年起便登台演出的舞狮团，将有约100名现任和前任成员参加2月5日的第18届农历新年大游行，与另外220个团体一同穿越曼哈顿唐人街。

paragraphs.en: Mr. Fong has been involved in the organization since he was 15 and began teaching lion dancing three years ago. But the club also acts as a recreation center and safe haven for teenagers, with video games readily available. As many as 20 students show up after school. "The basic requirement for hanging out here is you have to learn how to lion dance," Mr. Fong said. While that is not a formal requirement for being a member, everyone finds a way to participate in the Lunar New Year Parade, whether it's by dancing, carrying a flag or beating a drum.
paragraphs.zh: 方先生从15岁起就参与该组织，三年前开始教授舞狮。但这家俱乐部同时也是青少年的娱乐中心和避风港，这里随时都能玩电子游戏。放学后最多有20个学生会来这里。"在这儿待着的基本要求，就是必须学舞狮，"方先生说。虽然这并非入会的正式条件，但每个人都会找到参与农历新年游行的方式，无论是亲自下场舞狮、扛旗还是敲鼓。

paragraphs.en: Alvin Chau, aged 26, is an environmental consultant by day and a lion dancer on weekends. He has been a club member for 10 years and joined because of an interest in lion dance. "We're a big family," he said, shaking hands with other members as they walked through the door. "You know everyone."
paragraphs.zh: 26岁的阿尔文·周平日是环境顾问，周末则是一名舞狮者。他加入俱乐部已十年，当初是因为对舞狮感兴趣才来的。"我们是一个大家庭，"他一边说，一边与其他进门来的成员握手，"这里的每个人都彼此认识。"

paragraphs.en: It is believed that the lion dance began in the third century. Stories vary about how lion dancing came to be, but most of them include a monster named Nian who would terrorize a village. The villagers finally banded together and scared the beast away with firecrackers and drums. While lions are not native to China, some versions of the story include the villagers creating a monster of their own in the shape of a lion to fight off the beast.
paragraphs.zh: 人们认为舞狮起源于公元三世纪。关于舞狮的起源有各种传说，但大多数版本都提到了一只名为"年"的怪兽，它常常侵扰村庄。村民们最终团结起来，用鞭炮和鼓声吓跑了这只猛兽。虽然狮子并非中国本土物种，但有些传说版本说，村民们自己创造了一只狮子模样的怪物来对抗那只猛兽。

words.meaning: phr. 联合起来；团结起来

paragraphs.en: Today, the dancers travel across Chinatown going from business to business to bring good luck for the coming year. The new year — according to the Chinese lunar calendar — will begin on Saturday and marks the Year of the Rooster, which is thought to be a symbol of positivity. "It's almost like the dark night is ending and the sun is coming up," said Ya Yung Teng, the digital collections coordinator for the Museum of Chinese in America. "It's hopeful that we're going to have a new day." Roosters and chickens are not particularly strong creatures, Ms. Teng said, but they are numerous. "In a way," she said, the rooster "stands for 'We the People.'"
paragraphs.zh: 如今，舞狮者穿梭于唐人街，挨家挨户为商家送上新一年的好运。根据中国农历，新的一年从周六开始，标志着鸡年的到来，鸡被视为积极向上的象征。美国华人博物馆数字藏品协调员邓雅雍说："这感觉就像是漫长的黑夜即将结束，太阳正要升起。我们对即将迎来的新一天充满希望。"邓女士说，公鸡和小鸡并非特别强壮的生物，但它们数量众多。"从某种意义上说，"她说，鸡"代表着'我们人民'。"

paragraphs.en: The lion head and tail are operated by two people. As one person controls the head, a second follows under a train of fabric representing the body. The dancers move in harmony as they imitate the animal's natural movements in the wild, including the aggressiveness of an attack. A lion head figure weighs under 10 pounds and sits squarely on the dancer's shoulders. Inside, the dancer manipulates strings that open and close the lion's eyes, shake its ears and open its mouth to reveal a fire-orange tongue.
paragraphs.zh: 狮头和狮尾由两人共同操作。一人在前面控制头部，另一人则藏身于代表狮身的布幔之下紧随其后。舞者动作协调一致，模仿着狮子在野外的自然姿态，包括发起攻击时的凶猛。一个狮头道具重量不足10磅，稳稳地架在舞者的肩膀上。在狮头内部，舞者通过操纵绳索来控制狮子眼睛的开合、耳朵的晃动以及嘴巴的张启，露出火橙色的舌头。

paragraphs.en: "A good lion dancer will imitate a living creature," said Karlin Chan, aged 59, who heads the athletic club's community public relations. "I started lion dancing when I was a kid," he said. "Chinatown was much smaller then and it was a celebration with fireworks and firecrackers, which added a lot of flavor and meaning to it."
paragraphs.zh: "一个好的舞狮者能模仿出活物的神韵，"59岁的俱乐部社区公关负责人卡林·陈说。"我从小就开始舞狮，"他说，"那时候唐人街小得多，庆祝活动会放烟花鞭炮，给舞狮增添了许多味道和意义。"

paragraphs.en: Mr. Chan buys a new lion head each year when he travels to China. A head costs about $1,500. "If you want the good stuff, the quality, you have to see it for yourself," he said. "I'll inspect the product before we put it in the container and ship it over." Mr. Chan, who has been involved with the club for nearly 50 years, said that passing the dance from one generation to the next was vital. "You need to pass on the traditions and the culture, and this is a part of our culture," he said. "It's a great way to promote cultural understanding and exchange; we welcome that."
paragraphs.zh: 陈先生每年回中国时都会采购一个新的狮头。一个狮头大约要1500美元。"如果你想要好货，想要品质，就得亲自去看，"他说，"我会在装柜发货前亲自检查。"与俱乐部结缘近五十年的陈先生说，将这门技艺代代相传至关重要。"你需要传承传统和文化，而舞狮正是我们文化的一部分，"他说，"这也是促进文化理解与交流的绝佳途径，我们乐见其成。"

paragraphs.en: For Sara Pore, aged 17, another club member, lion dance is more than just tradition; it provides a creative outlet. "Lion dancing started 2,000 years ago — that's incredible," she said. "But what makes you a competent lion dancer is that there's a sense of imagination involved. Lion dancing teaches competence in leadership because of this. You're constantly forced to push yourself past your limit."
paragraphs.zh: 对于俱乐部另一位17岁的成员莎拉·波尔来说，舞狮不仅是传统，更是一种发挥创造力的途径。"舞狮起源于两千年前——这太不可思议了，"她说，"但要成为一名称职的舞狮者，关键要融入想象力。正因为如此，舞狮能教会你领导能力。你不断被逼着突破自己的极限。"

paragraphs.en: Back at rehearsal, Justin Le, aged 18, tied a red ribbon around his waist to practice jumping. The dancers use the ribbons to pull themselves up over their partners' heads. The room's ceilings are too low to wear the lion heads for jumping practice, so once up on his partner's head, Mr. Le held out his arms as if in offering.
paragraphs.zh: 回到排练现场，18岁的贾斯汀·乐在腰间系上红绸带练习跳跃。舞者们借助这些绸带将自己拉起来，越过搭档的头顶。由于房间天花板太低，没法戴着狮头练习跳跃，乐先生一跳到搭档头上，便伸出双臂，做出仿佛献礼的姿势。

paragraphs.en: Mr. Le comes by the dance as a legacy. "I was born into it," Mr. Le said, noting that his uncle and father were club members. "Growing up, I would always watch my family and see the lion dance, and I slowly grew interested in it." By the age of 14, he was fully enrolled in the athletic club's lion dance group. "I value my culture and tradition, being Chinese or Asian-American. I have a lot of pride in that," he said. "I want to contribute and give back to the community."
paragraphs.zh: 乐先生与舞狮的缘分源自家族传承。"我生来就是干这个的，"乐先生说，他提到自己的叔叔和父亲都是俱乐部成员。"从小到大，我总是看着家人和舞狮表演，慢慢就产生了兴趣。"到14岁时，他已正式加入体育俱乐部的舞狮队。"身为华人或亚裔美国人，我珍视自己的文化和传统。我对此深感自豪，"他说，"我想为社区做贡献，回馈社区。"
